name: Yellow bell pepper (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (concentration_mM assumes 100 g ≈ 100 mL, density ≈1)

components.name: water
components.evidence: raw sweet yellow pepper ~92% water

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: free glucose 2.0 g/100g; co-dominant fermentable sugar with fructose

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: free fructose 2.30 g/100g; dominant sugar. Fructose>glucose>>sucrose is typical for ripe (yellow/red) Capsicum annuum

components.name: sucrose
components.source: Antonio et al. 2009 PMID 19235156 (Capsicum, sucrose 0–150 mg/100g); Ciqual total sugars 4.30 = glucose+fructose
components.evidence: sucrose is near-zero in ripe pepper (declines during ripening as hexoses accumulate); total sugars 4.30 g/100g fully accounted for by glucose+fructose

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Wahyuni et al. PMC9871545 (Capsicum annuum, citric 1.82–5.60 mg/g FW = 182–560 mg/100g); Antonio 2009 PMID 19235156 (0–818 mg/100g)
components.evidence: dominant non-sugar organic acid (39–64% of total organic acids); citrate rises during ripening so yellow (ripe) is in the upper part of the range. Midpoint ~400 mg/100g used; range 0.18–0.56 g/100g

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Wahyuni et al. PMC9871545 (malic 0.99–1.88 mg/g FW = 99–188 mg/100g)
components.evidence: 3rd most abundant organic acid (13–22% of total); malate DECLINES during ripening so ripe yellow fruit sits at the lower end. Range 0.10–0.19 g/100g

components.name: fumaric acid
components.source: Wahyuni et al. PMC9871545 (fumaric 1.48–3.71 mg/g FW = 148–371 mg/100g)
components.evidence: reported as 2nd most abundant organic acid (19–34% of total) in that Capsicum annuum survey; midpoint ~230 mg/100g used. Value is dataset-dependent (fumarate can also be an analytical/derived pool) – flag as high-uncertainty

components.name: alpha-ketoglutaric acid (2-oxoglutarate)
components.source: Wahyuni et al. PMC9871545 (α-ketoglutarate 0.70–1.43 mg/g FW = 70–143 mg/100g)
components.evidence: 10–17% of total organic acids; TCA-cycle intermediate usable as C/energy source

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: Wahyuni et al. PMC9871545 (oxalic <0.7 mg/g FW = <70 mg/100g)
components.evidence: minor organic acid (<8% of total)

components.name: L-ascorbate (vitamin C)
components.source: USDA FDC 169383 (183.5 mg); Ciqual/Aprifel gives 121 mg
components.evidence: yellow bell pepper is exceptionally vitamin-C rich; also a genuine organic acid. Range ~120–184 mg/100g by database

components.name: dehydroascorbate
components.source: FooDB (present, unquantified); oxidised form of ascorbate
components.evidence: present as the oxidised ascorbate pool; not separately quantified

components.name: protein (total)
components.source: Ciqual/Aprifel yellow pepper; USDA FDC 169383 ~1.0 g
components.evidence: low protein; amino acids below are the USDA-derived protein-bound profile

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: most abundant amino acid; aspartate/asparagine is the major free amino-N pool in pepper

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: 2nd most abundant; major free amino acid and N source

components.name: L-citrulline
components.evidence: genuine free amino acid in Capsicum; not quantified by USDA

components.name: gamma-aminobutyrate (GABA)
components.evidence: GABA is a notable free amino acid in bell pepper; not quantified here

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: dominant mineral

components.name: phosphorus
components.evidence: P source

components.name: sodium
components.evidence: very low sodium

components.name: iron
components.evidence: covers Fe2+/Fe3+ pools listed in FooDB

components.name: selenium
components.source: USDA green sweet pepper analog (USDA FDC 170427)
components.evidence: trace essential micronutrient; yellow not separately assayed, green analog used

components.name: molybdate (molybdenum)
components.evidence: trace essential micronutrient (retained as genuine trace mineral)

components.name: vitamin C (see L-ascorbate above)
components.evidence: duplicate pointer – same pool as L-ascorbate

components.name: niacin (vitamin B3)
components.evidence: covers nicotinate pool

components.name: pyridoxine (vitamin B6)
components.source: Ciqual/Aprifel (0.079 mg); USDA green analog ~0.22 mg

components.name: choline
components.source: USDA green sweet pepper analog (USDA FDC 170427 ~5.5 mg)
components.evidence: yellow not separately assayed; green analog

components.name: vitamin A (RAE)
components.source: Ciqual/Aprifel yellow pepper (RAE); provitamin from carotenoids

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: provitamin-A carotenoid

components.name: vitamin K (phylloquinone)
components.source: USDA green sweet pepper analog (USDA FDC 170427)
components.evidence: yellow not separately assayed; green analog used

components.name: total dietary fiber
components.evidence: largely non-fermentable structurally; cell-wall polysaccharide

components.name: total lipid (fat)
components.evidence: very low fat; pepper is not a fatty food

components.name: linoleic acid (C18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: dominant PUFA of the small lipid fraction

components.name: alpha-linolenic acid (C18:3 n-3)
components.evidence: minor PUFA

components.name: oleic acid (C18:1 n-9)
components.evidence: minor MUFA

remove.name: Silver
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: toxic metalloid contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Cadmium
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant

remove.name: Lead
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant

remove.name: Mercury
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element at contaminant levels

remove.name: Co2+ / Cobalt
remove.reason: trace; not a nutritive component of pepper (only relevant bound in B12, which plants lack)

remove.name: Creatine cytosol
remove.reason: animal metabolite, biologically implausible in a plant; FooDB predicted/mis-assigned

remove.name: Taurine C2H7NO3S
remove.reason: animal metabolite, implausible in a plant food; FooDB predicted

remove.name: 17alpha-ethynylestradiol
remove.reason: synthetic pharmaceutical estrogen, implausible in pepper; FooDB predicted contaminant

remove.name: N-Methylaniline
remove.reason: industrial aromatic amine, not a plant nutrient; contaminant/predicted

remove.name: p-Aminobenzaldehyde
remove.reason: implausible synthetic aromatic aldehyde; FooDB predicted noise

remove.name: Piperidine
remove.reason: not a genuine bell-pepper metabolite at nutritive level; predicted/trace

remove.name: Solanidine
remove.reason: Solanum (potato/tomato) steroidal alkaloid aglycone; mis-assigned to Capsicum – pepper glycoalkaloid is different

remove.name: Racemethionine
remove.reason: DL(racemic) methionine is a synthetic descriptor; duplicate of L-methionine already listed

remove.name: Aluminum
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element

remove.name: Titanium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element

remove.name: Strontium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element

remove.name: Barium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element

remove.name: Tin
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element

remove.name: Rubidium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element

remove.name: Bromine
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element

remove.name: Silicon
remove.reason: structural trace element, not a growth nutrient in a defined medium

remove.name: Boron
remove.reason: plant micronutrient but non-nutritive for microbial growth; trace

missing_added: citric acid (dominant organic acid, was absent)
quantified vitamins and minerals (K, P, Mg, Ca, Fe, Zn, etc.)
keep_uncovered: Capsaicin, Dihydrocapsaicin, Nordihydrocapsaicin, Homocapsaicin, Homodihydrocapsaicin, Nonivamide (capsaicinoids – genuine to Capsicum, near-zero in non-pungent bell pepper but real)
Capsianoside I–IV, A, C, D, E, F, II, III (genuine Capsicum acyclic diterpene glycosides)
Capsicoside A1/B1/C1 (genuine steroidal saponins of Capsicum)
Lutein, (3R,3'R,all-E)-Zeaxanthin, Beta-cryptoxanthin, Beta-carotene (carotenoids – yellow pepper is lutein/zeaxanthin/violaxanthin rich)
Caffeic acid, Ferulate, Gallic acid, Vanillate, 2,3-Dihydroxybenzoate (phenolic acids)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169383 (Peppers, sweet, yellow, raw)
USDA FoodData Central FDC 170427 (Peppers, sweet, green, raw) – analog for Se, choline, vitamin K
Ciqual / Aprifel yellow pepper nutritional sheet
nutritionvalue.org (USDA-derived) – yellow sweet pepper amino-acid profile
Wahyuni et al., comprehensive Capsicum annuum fruit-quality / organic-acid study (PMC9871545)
Antonio et al. 2009, Variability for free sugars and organic acids in Capsicum chinense (PMID 19235156)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources: free hexoses fructose (~2.3 g) + glucose (~2.0 g), with essentially no sucrose in this ripe (yellow) fruit; the leading organic-acid carbon source is citric acid (~0.4 g/100g, rises with ripening) followed by malic (~0.12 g, falls with ripening), fumaric, alpha-ketoglutarate and oxalic. Yellow bell pepper is exceptionally vitamin-C-rich (~120–184 mg/100g). Removed 24 FooDB noise entries (heavy metals, non-nutritive trace elements, and implausible animal/synthetic compounds); amino acids are the USDA protein-bound profile and organic-acid amounts are literature midpoints (ranges given per component). Fumaric acid value carries higher uncertainty.
